scope: Final read-only convergence audit of the current combined tree after Cycle 2 Round 1/2 remediation, QA, performance, and visionary work.

findings.title: A confirmation-token collision can replace the action the operator previewed
findings.evidence: WRITE confirmation tokens use uuid.uuid4().hex[:8] (32 bits) and are assigned directly to _pending without collision detection. A deterministic repeated-UUID probe staged two different actions under deadbeef; confirming the older visible token executed only the newer action.
findings.exploitability: Normal human-use collision probability is negligible, but an in-process future ARIA integration can stage tens of thousands of actions within the five-minute TTL and create meaningful 32-bit birthday-collision pressure.
findings.impact: An operator responding to an older preview can execute the newer individually valid callback and arguments stored under the same visible token.
findings.remediation: Use at least 128 bits of visible token entropy, allocate with a collision-retry loop without overwriting live tokens, and add one deterministic forced-collision regression; add a common lock if ARIA becomes multi-threaded.

verified_without_new_regression: Response Safety Kernel remains shadow-only with no authoritative consumer
interruptible and bounded Red Team/Shark cancellation
exact-path process trust for path-rich events
AAR exact-path or PID-plus-token bounded correlation
D-drive runtime, direct-selfcheck temp, drill, and verification storage
no new crash or production watchdog signature
